review_text: Delicious cappuccino and Milky Way Latte. However, the bagel sandwiches leave a lot to be desired. The portion size is too small for the price, smaller than the size of my hand. They skimp on the ingredients too. The bagel sandwiches should be $5-$6 at most for what you are being served, but they charge $9-$10. Not the best bagels I have had either. I would only recommend buying the bagels on their own without toppings. Otherwise, it’s not worth it and you will leave feeling ripped off. To note: Food safety rating is “Okay”, which is one of the lowest I’ve seen for restaurants. There are plants sitting in mold at the tables and cobwebs around. The place is popular, especially for early morning in a Sunday. Not sure why based on my experience.

review_text: Matcha was either lower grade or not executed very well. The employees were dismissive from the moment we stepped in and seemed to barely make an effort to properly whisk the matcha prior to serving. They did ask awkwardly if I cared what kind of milk I’d like which should have been done when taking the order or made to standard if unspecified. The end product was a chalky, clumpy yellow green matcha that could have been made at home using Costco matcha. Experience could have been better if: 1 - employees taking order looked like they wanted to take the order 2 - if we set a standard milk choice and or ask for preference during order 3 - use a better quality matcha that doesn’t have a gritty mouthfeel 4 - use proper whisking technique or ditch the show and just blend it like Starbucks.

review_text: I rather give this place a ZERO STAR - Here is what happened today - My friends and I have been coming to Ding Tea on the Ave for years as our go-to spot to relax after lunch. Today, though, was a complete letdown. We were casually browsing the menu and chatting when the owner, Kang Han ( I don't know the exact spelling but he is indeed the owner of Ding Tea on on the Ave), came over and demanded we order immediately or leave. His tone was rude and condescending, and the whole interaction felt aggressive and completely uncalled for. Believe me, I had been going to the Ave for over 20+ years. I went to school (UW) here and never been treated ever by any business like this on the AVE. We told him we were regulars—one of us has even tipped 25% in the past—but he scoffed and said he’s always at the store and doesn’t recognize us. Then, in a passive-aggressive move, he pointed at a sign saying “we have the right to refuse service to anyone,” which felt like a slap in the face. This kind of behavior from an owner is baffling. It’s as if he's actively trying to drive customers away. Maybe Seattle got to him—or maybe he genuinely doesn’t care about the business anymore. Either way, we won’t be coming back, and if you value being treated with basic respect, you might want to think twice before going yourself. I have also been to a numerous number of boba tea places and there is nothing special about this place. Good riddance.

Their menu has a great variety, so there’s always something new to try, but the classics are just as good every single time. Whether you’re craving milk tea, fruit tea, or something refreshing, Happy Lemon always hits the spot. Highly recommend!

review_text: My family are frequent customers of Happy Lemon Redmond location. By that, I meant coming here almost weekly for two years. We love Happy Lemon in general and have gone to Bellevue square, Issaquah, and U village locations as well in the past five years. The Redmond location has consistent, perfect boba texture and sweetness. Drink qualities are also more consistent compared to other locations (which caused us to come to this location more frequently than any other locations) The staffs here are also very welcoming, efficient, and easy to communicate! Because we come so frequently, yes, there are rare occasions where drinks weren't made right, but the staffs handle the situation well and remake drinks efficiently. There are also times where I accidentally forgot to order additional topping or adjust sugar/ice levels and the staffs were quick to help us revise our orders.

review_text: I’m a frequent customer of Happy Lemon at the Bellevue location and usually have a great experience. However, my recent visit to the Redmond Way location was disappointing. I ordered a Hazelnut Choco Milk, which normally comes with whipped cream, a proper lid, and a longer straw. Initially, the staff mentioned there was no long straw available, but someone later went to the back and provided one. After receiving the drink, we left the store. On the way home, I realized the drink didn’t have any boba at all. When we returned, they added boba into my already half-finished cup and handed it back. Overall, the service felt careless and inconsistent, which was surprising given my positive experiences at other locations. I hope this location improves attention to orders and service quality.

review_text: 10/10, def will come back! Perfect for tea lovers and matcha drinkers who prefer their matcha strong & authentic. I got the woodland latte and as a tea lover, I highly recommend it! It had a perfect blend of jasmine tea flavor with warm hearty milk and just the right amount of sweetness. My friend got the ceremonial matcha which she said was super strong so as a strong matcha drinker myself, I would love to try it next time! They have some tea out on the counter that’s free to samples which I absolutely loved! There’s some desks and chairs in the cafe but be warned this is a very popular cafe (and rightfully so with their wonderful tea and kind service) so don’t always bet on getting a place to sit at every time you go.

review_text: Moge Tee shares its space with Nan Noodles, and it’s a surprisingly great combo with a clean, spacious, and inviting interior. The cozy booths and warm lighting make it an easy place to hang out despite the strip-mall location. The drinks are refreshing, well-balanced, and generously sized without being overly sweet. Ordering is done through a kiosk, service is friendly, and everything comes out quickly. Prices are slightly higher than average, but the quality makes it worth coming back.

Neither are over sweet, though the smoothie is definitely as sweet as you would think a strawberry smoothie would be. The vibe of this place is also really fun, the workers are really friendly, and everything was done super quickly! I saw a lot of complaints about price in their reviews, but i think for the quality and size it’s definitely a fair price, 6$ for a 16/20oz iced drink is very much standard! review_text: Hyson is a local neighborhood gem for me. As a bubble tea snob, there are a lot of things I consider when trying a new bubble tea place. I think the most important thing to note is that if you are looking for very authentic taiwanese tasting bubble tea, this place probably isn't the perfect fit. However, this is personally the best bubble tea option in my opinion in the Madison valley area. The flavors are super unique and experimental and they have awesome seasonal flavors. This is the place you go too when you're wanting a fun new drink! The espresso based drinks though are where it personally shines for me! The PB&J oat espresso and the banana pistachio are divine. The owner is the sweetest person ever. My in-laws went 3 times over the span of 2 weeks truly because of the great customer service. Boba's are made in-house and all of the flavors are real fruit and no artificial syrups. This has become one of my top favorite local neighborhood shops!

review_text: Such a cute shop inside Factoria mall! I got the red bean egg waffle and the rose milk tea with mochi and really enjoyed it! The egg waffle was perfectly crispy on the edges and chewy and soft in the inside, I also really like how there was real red beans inside. The egg waffle is definitely on the plainer/less sweet side so if you’re a sweet tooth I definitely recommend getting the dipping sauce with it. Meanwhile the rose milk tea was very fragrant and refreshing. The rose taste is strong and is perfect for someone craving that floral taste. The cloud mochi was the perfect topping and I really liked the texture of it. The store itself is very cute and they sell Rolife blind boxes and DIY houses, in addition to having Japanese style claw machines in the back. Will be back when in the area!

review_text: Macy Tea’s mango avocado smoothie is the kind of drink that instantly puts you in a good mood—creamy, refreshing, and naturally sweet in a way that feels both healthy and delicious at the same time. The mango brings a bright, tropical flavor, while the avocado makes it extra smooth and rich without feeling heavy. On top of that, the environment at Macy Tea is super relaxing and welcoming. It’s clean, cozy, and has a calm vibe that makes you want to sit down, chill, and enjoy your drink. Whether you’re stopping by to study, hang out, or just grab something tasty, the whole experience feels really good.

review_text: This cafe only makes sense if you’re getting the Kung fu tea (pure loose leaf tea). The popular Jasmin silk latte is fragrant but it lacks the characteristic creaminess that a milk tea has and that boba chains get right. It tastes really just like mixing a tea bag with milk at home, which I comforting but maybe I can save $7 for. The jasmine latte at Heytea a couple blocks over is so much more fragrant, for example. But it’s a cute local place tucked away in the plaza with indoor and outdoor seating. They don’t have free tastings or samples everyday fyi so I didn’t get to try their other teas. Overall it’s a 3.5/5 for me.

review_text: Seattle Bubble Tea Gem! Happy Lemon in Seattle, never disappoints! Their bubble tea is consistently fresh, flavorful, and perfectly balanced—not too sweet, just right. The salted cheese drinks are a must-try, especially the jasmine green tea version—rich, creamy, and so refreshing. The tapioca pearls are chewy and perfectly cooked every time. Staff are friendly and efficient, and the shop has a bright, cheerful vibe that makes waiting a pleasure. Whether you're grabbing a quick pick-me-up or chilling with friends, Happy Lemon is always a great choice. Highly recommended for any boba lover!

review_text: So much better than Starbucks! Love that I can get a taste of unique, excellent local coffee at the airport instead of a big chain. Their specialty lattes are all amazing. The Ube Latte, Black Sesame, Mint Mojito and Coconut matcha are ones I tried as a latte flight (iced) at their brick and mortar before and I loved all of them. Just had an Ube Latte (hot) again at the airport location an it hit the spot. Perfect balance of sweetness and espresso. I also got the breakfast sandwich and could tell it was homemade. They even offer mobile ordering so you can order for pickup ahead of time. Highly recommend if you’re flying out of SeaTac and want to try something authentic.

review_text: I ordered a hot black sesame latte with soy milk through the kiosk today(Thursday around 4 pm) , and the service was unacceptable. First, my drink came out cold instead of hot. When I pointed it out, the staff acted surprised and said he’d remake it. After waiting another 5 minutes, I was handed a cappuccino with regular milk - completely wrong and unsafe for me as someone who is lactose intolerant. I even paid an extra $1.10 for soy milk and asked for verbal confirmation since there was no sticker on the cup. Despite that, I ended up with the wrong drink, took one sip when i reached my gate, and immediately reacted to the milk. The entire $8 went straight into the trash. This isn’t just poor service - it’s careless and risky. If a café can’t get basic orders right, especially when allergies and intolerances are involved, it’s not worth the money or the health risk. People with intolerances steer clear - getting your drink right from this shop seems like extreme sports.

Despite my polite inquiry, he flatly refused twice without any professional courtesy. ​Due to his hostile behavior and lack of basic respect, I cancelled the delivery on the spot. ​Delivery personnel are the bridge between your shop and your customers. By treating us with such contempt, you are directly sabotaging your own business. My cancellation means the end customer now has to wait significantly longer for their order, all because of one staff member's arrogance. A restroom should be available to those facilitating your sales, not used as a way to power trip. This employee is a liability to your brand image, and I strongly urge management to take disciplinary action. This level of service is absolutely unacceptable."

review_text: I personally would not recommend this space. Immediately going in, I was directed to the self-serve kiosks and told to order from there. I understand that the workers might have been busy at around night time but creating a welcoming space and not being told without question to go to the self-serve tablets would’ve been nice. Once I got my drink, I found the taste fairly yummy but most of all difficult to drink. If you get a drink with seeds in them, expect to be crunching on those bitter seeds all night making up most of the drink experience than anything else. I ended up throwing away my drink 1/3 completed because it was too difficult to enjoy. If there is an option to drink it without all of the seeds, I think that’d be nice! Other than that, the space is well decorated and has lots of open space to enjoy your boba with friends. They also provide games too and a bathroom! However, because of the service and distaste for the drink, it was not one of my favorite spots as a visitor in Washington.

review_text: Snoqualmie Ice Cream in Kirkland delivers the kind of experience that reminds you why dessert shops become local legends. The ice cream itself is the star, rich, velvety, and crafted with a level of care you can taste immediately. Today’s lineup was especially impressive. The salted caramel, topped with whipped cream, nuts, and an extra drizzle of caramel sauce, hit that perfect balance of sweet, salty, and indulgent. Every bite felt intentional. The dark chocolate was equally outstanding—deep, smooth, and satisfying with the whipped cream adding just the right lift. The only real drawback is the price. At $24 before tip for two sundaes, it’s definitely on the higher end for a casual treat. Still, the quality is undeniable, and if you’re in the mood to splurge on something genuinely exceptional, Snoqualmie Ice Cream makes it feel worth it. A premium experience with premium pricing, delicious, memorable, and a great stop when you want to treat yourself.

review_text: I rather give this place a ZERO STAR - Here is what happened today - My friends and I have been coming to Ding Tea on the Ave for years as our go-to spot to relax after lunch. Today, though, was a complete letdown. We were casually browsing the menu and chatting when the owner, Kang Han ( I don't know the exact spelling but he is indeed the owner of Ding Tea on on the Ave), came over and demanded we order immediately or leave. His tone was rude and condescending, and the whole interaction felt aggressive and completely uncalled for. Believe me, I had been going to the Ave for over 20+ years. I went to school (UW) here and never been treated ever by any business like this on the AVE. We told him we were regulars—one of us has even tipped 25% in the past—but he scoffed and said he’s always at the store and doesn’t recognize us. Then, in a passive-aggressive move, he pointed at a sign saying “we have the right to refuse service to anyone,” which felt like a slap in the face. This kind of behavior from an owner is baffling. It’s as if he's actively trying to drive customers away. Maybe Seattle got to him—or maybe he genuinely doesn’t care about the business anymore. Either way, we won’t be coming back, and if you value being treated with basic respect, you might want to think twice before going yourself. I have also been to a numerous number of boba tea places and there is nothing special about this place. Good riddance.

review_text: A Taiwan-born tea stand that started in Taichung and has expanded across Asia and into the U.S. They offer an impressive lineup of around 50 drinks, all made with carefully selected tea leaves used generously, so the tea flavor really stands out. You can customize sweetness, ice level, and toppings, which makes it easy to find a drink that perfectly matches your taste. Whether you prefer something classic or more adventurous, there’s plenty to choose from. A great spot for high-quality Taiwanese tea!

review_text: This has to be one of the funnest names for a boba shop. As a self-proclaimed boba connoisseur (maybe just an addict), I was impressed with my drink, vibe, and name of this place. I ordered their Osmanthus Jasmine Milk Tea with Oat Milk and Brown Sugar Boba since I was feeling a little bit florally. Loved that they offered non-dairy milk options although it's nearly $1 more. The world needs to change this and just offer it as an option for those that can't have dairy. My drink was fantastic. Beverage itself is delicious and you can taste the quality of the brewed tea. Boba was cooked to perfection and use of brown sugar is a nice touch instead of the usual honey that I see, making the drink fully vegan. Don't worry - I won't be yelling at you anytime soon. Unless you enjoy being yelled at for good boba, then here I come.

review_text: My partner and I like the way tea was made in this location better than in Bellevue one, despite this location being smaller in size. However, in the past few months, we’ve noticed a difference in quality. We used to get more cream milk foam topping, now it’s barely floating on top. The sugar levels are lower: the current 75% on 4 seasons milk tea feels closer to 50%. We have asked the staff to adjust the sugar level when we noticed it the first time, and it was fixed. However, on the second time the sugar levels were off, the drink didn’t become sweeter. We suspect it was because on the second time, the brown boba balls weren’t sweet either, like they usually are. When we have asked to adjust the sugar in our second encounter, the employee took off the plastic wrap from the cup, and replaced with a plastic lid. The straw, however, wasn’t tall enough to fully emerge from the cup lid, which made drinking boba awkward. review_text: I stopped by for Nom Nom Sando and while I was waiting for our order, I figured I should stop by and try out this spot too! There were so many different options which was neat and I picked one I never usually see which was their Crispy Apple Iced tea. There wasn't any customization options besides adding in a topping which is unfortunate as I always request light ice and less sweetness just in case. Additionally, I wasn't sure how big the cup was or anything. Other than that, my drink was made relatively quickly and I think I was their first order that say as my order number said 1. The drink was pretty and it was pretty tasty especially with the sandos next to them. Unfortunately, it was filled to the brim with ice and there wasn't much to drink. They share a space with another business so there is plenty of seating but it's more on the Nom Nom Sando side with two tables and a long bench near the entrance of the side of Yan Tea. So not sure if you can sit on the Nom Nom Sando side. Not much to say on the service as they have a kiosk to order on. The employee will just call your number and name to get your drink and not say much else. I do like that as an introvert but it would have been nice to have a nice day or a response to my thank you. Might come back to try in the future but there are also other drink places nearby where I think I would rather try. Edit and response to owner: I appreciate the detailed response and honesty! I've edited my review to three stars now that I have something understanding to the issues that I've encountered during my visit. It would have been nice to known about the information that you provided me about the Apple series on the menu or a note to let me know that was the case. Transparency and information goes a long way but there wasn't even descriptions of what apples or tea was used. As a fellow introvert, I totally understand and I prefer minimal conversation but just a response to my thank you would have been enough for me. I wish you luck with the service training and success of your business!

review_text: 36 Streets Vietnamese Coffee is a charming new spot in the Kirkland area, conveniently located near Juanita Beach Park. They open early at 8 AM, perfect for grabbing a morning coffee and breakfast. Parking can be a bit limited right in front of the store, but there are free parking lots nearby where you can usually find a spot. The shop is small, with only a few tables and chairs inside and out, so it’s more of a grab-and-go kind of place. However, if you do choose to stay, the cozy Vietnamese-inspired decor creates a pleasant atmosphere. For those who understand Vietnamese, they even have a selection of comics to read while you enjoy your coffee. The menu is simple, focusing on traditional Vietnamese coffee and breakfast items. The staff is friendly and helpful, offering recommendations if you’re new to Vietnamese cuisine. We tried a variety of their offerings, including the egg coffee, salt coffee, hot condensed milk coffee, Vietnamese sandwich, and bubble waffle. A word of caution—the coffee here is quite strong, much stronger than typical American coffee. Just half a cup had my heart racing, which isn’t something I’ve experienced even with coffee in Vietnam. The flavor is rich and authentic, so if you enjoy a strong brew, you’ll likely appreciate it. The Vietnamese sandwich is a solid breakfast choice, filling and well-paired with the coffee. Just be aware that it comes with jalapeños by default, so ask them to leave it out if you’re not a fan. The bubble waffle is decent, on par with other places, and they also offer some boba options that I plan to try next time. Overall, it’s exciting to have a Vietnamese breakfast spot in Kirkland, adding a unique and flavorful option to the local scene. If you’re in the area and looking for a strong coffee and a satisfying breakfast, 36 Streets is definitely worth a visit.

review_text: This establishment offers natural bubble drinks, specifically fruit-flavored varieties with unique, handmade toppings. It primarily targets teenagers and young children, as evidenced by the inclusion of gummy candies as a topping option. The drinks tend to be very sweet, which may appeal to younger tastes but are somewhat too sugary for an adult palate. Nonetheless, many customers find the offerings satisfying and well-suited to their preferences. Despite this, the shop is known for its excellent customer service and a welcoming, lively environment inside.

review_text: Clean store, and kind staff. Order was made in a timely manner. Roasted oolong milk tea was yummy but we ordered it hot and it came out luke warm which threw me off (they had a warm vs hot option and we chose hot). I honestly should’ve probably said something but I didn’t want to force them to remake it since it still tasted good. The mochi waffles were freshly made to order and tasted delicious with the cream on the side. You get 3 waffles in an order which is great in my opinion! My biggest issue was the smell. As soon as I walked in there was a pungent smell, I couldn’t even describe it to you. My nose got used to it after about 10 minutes but it did make me slightly nauseous. All in all, it’s worth giving the place a shot. Knocking down a star for the smell and the luke warm tea but I’d try it again.

review_text: I saw this place online and decided to give it a try. I’d say it was one of the best strawberry matcha I’ve had, and I’ve tried A LOT. This place was obviously super popular because the line went outside the door when we went, but we got our order faster than expected. And since it was so busy, it was very hard to find a table/place to sit. And it was also super loud as well so keep that in mind. Definitely NOT a work/study cafe. The matcha had a nice and potent. Strawberry puree the bottom + cream top = perfect combo. Every single cup of matcha was hand-whisked too. My bf got a hot hojicha and he really liked it too. I’m not a big fan of hojicha so I have no comment. Overall, I’d definitely come back here again. Prices were a bit high compared to the cup size, but with how good my drink was it was worth it to me.

review_text: Me and a bunch of people went for the neopets x Gong Cha collaboration. A handful of us waited outside hours before opening. The owner was not expecting the turnout, but listened to the first people in line and went to grab the last of the plushies that had been left behind. We also asked her if she could put a limit to how many each person could buy, because we knew there were a lot of neopets fans in line, and some obvious scalpers. The company didn’t create a limit, but the owner said she would, which meant that even though they sold out very quickly, more people were able to enjoy them. She even mixed the boxes up when we asked, since we heard from people who bought them on the east coast that the blind boxes weren’t shuffled at all and most people got duplicates. Even with the chaos, the employees were able to get things going smoothly and quickly. As for the drinks themselves, I got two (regular milk tea and mango green tea), and for a chain they weren’t bad!

review_text: Billing and Enforcement Issues Driven by a ‘Partner, Not Customer’ Model: After time and reflection, I am leaving this review to provide transparency for other food entrepreneurs. During our membership, we experienced repeated billing inconsistencies, including invoices represented as unpaid after full payment and credits confirmed in writing that were not applied, resulting in delinquency notices despite documentation. Operationally, management of shared space was also problematic. Paid-for, dedicated refrigeration space was accessed by other members, and scheduled kitchen time slots were not consistently honored, disrupting production. When escalated, management intervention was not consistently provided. These issues were compounded by management’s stated view that members were “partners,” not customers, a framing that appeared to reduce accountability for customer service, billing resolution, and enforcement, while leaving operational and financial risk with the member. Additional concerns included ingredient security, use of private equipment by others, and inconsistent enforcement of posted rules. These conditions directly impacted production schedules and profitability, which is especially risky for small food businesses. This was not a single dispute, but a pattern. Prospective members should get everything in writing and carefully evaluate whether this environment supports their business needs as paying customers.

review_text: I’ve never understood the hype around matcha- I’ve tried it a few times and have always come away feeling like it just tasted like a $10 wet lawn smoothie. However- my crew stopped by for coffee after wedding dress shopping in Seattle. There was a tasting bar outside that had a few samples- including the banana matcha. That sample convinced me to order a 16oz banana matcha with cold foam- and I happily drank the whole thing. I feel like a convert! The golden glow (that my maid of honor ordered) was also really yummy too. Such a cute space- will definitely visit again!

review_text: This cafe only makes sense if you’re getting the Kung fu tea (pure loose leaf tea). The popular Jasmin silk latte is fragrant but it lacks the characteristic creaminess that a milk tea has and that boba chains get right. It tastes really just like mixing a tea bag with milk at home, which I comforting but maybe I can save $7 for. The jasmine latte at Heytea a couple blocks over is so much more fragrant, for example. But it’s a cute local place tucked away in the plaza with indoor and outdoor seating. They don’t have free tastings or samples everyday fyi so I didn’t get to try their other teas. Overall it’s a 3.5/5 for me.

review_text: Service is really good, atmosphere is very good, but to be honest, the tea doesn't taste good at all. My bf first ordered a hot oolong milk tea, came out not too hot and doesn't have much tea flavor, so he asked them to make him a new one with jasmine tea instead, came out luke warm and not much tea flavor, so I went back and told them, the guy was very nice and told me their machine was broken, so they have to heat it up with microwave. Finally, its hot, I tried it. It doesn't have much tea flavor and it just tasted like water down milk with a little tea flavor. I dont think I will go back to order anymore. I ordered the Dai Hong Pao milk tea, it was ok, still not much tea flavored, very light.

review_text: What a cool place to get milk, tea, Matcha, and other sweet drinks in Bellevue! The service is very friendly, they have a lot of drinks that you can’t get elsewhere! The drinks were still in the sweeter side with a reduction in the sweetness level. The drink was very good and I enjoyed the flavor and texture. I would recommend it would come back.

review_text: A relatively new tea shop that opened across the street from the Hmart and near the Amazon offices. They have a good selection of milk teas and tea lattes with a limited selection of pure tea options (only 4 options I think). There’s also a selection of cheese foam tea drinks where you can ask to remove the cheese foam if you’re lactose sensitive. They also have a large selection of toppings, even some less common ones such as taro paste, taro balls, aloe, and 寒天. Visited on a regular weekend afternoon and the place was PACKED. Even though there were a lot of seating options there were no seats available.

review_text: Good environment and friendly cashier. If you want to get points please add your phone number before you pay. I got Jasmine Tea Latte (contain pecan and cream), Milk tea with purple rice, and Orange fruit green tea. All are amazing tastes. Use spoon to eat the cream and pecan first and drink the jasmine tea latte. So yummy~ I will come back for other drinks.

review_text: Even after attending both days of the Northwest Tea Festival, I found that the vast majority of my teas still came from Tea Fairy. If you're looking for looseleaf teas and tea cakes from Mainland China, it's hard to go wrong with the selection here.

review_text: I got into Chinese tea in the gongfu style this year, and I was really happy to find a Teahouse on the East side! They actually helped me pick out my first (and current favorite) tea cake. I've been shopping around both online and in Seattle-area-local shops, and the tea from here has consistently been among my favorite. I've also been attending their tea classes, and it's been a really nice way to learn a little bit while also being able to try a wide variety of tea. We often end up just chatting over tea and some snacks by the end of it. The owner and staff are really friendly, and the tea is absolutely amazing. I highly recommend visiting if you have any interest in Chinese tea.

review_text: Finally found a top-quality tea shop in the Seattle area! This shop offers all six major categories of Chinese tea, selected by the owner. She has 20 years of experience in the tea industry. I’ve been tried black tea and Puer here. It’s really amazing!! After tasting the tea here, I went home and threw away all the tea bags I had bought from the grocery stores —they just don’t compare. Drinking high-quality loose-leaf tea here made me realize how rich and complex real tea flavors can be. Huge thanks to the owner for opening my eyes to a whole new level of tea appreciation!

review_text: The very best Cantonese lemon tea place and pineapple bun covered waffle eggs 🍳 The best of the best, healing me through the darkness weekdays and always reminding me with the homey food!

review_text: Back for the second time! The 菠萝包鸡蛋仔 is excellent. I’ve tried the Oreo Bubble Waffle before, which was also good, but the 菠萝包鸡蛋仔 is definitely better. As for the drinks, the Earl Grey Cheese Foam Milk Tea, Camellia Milk Tea, and Duck Shit Oolong (Ya Shi Xiang) Milk Tea were all solid. They aren't the 'mind-blowing' type, but they aren't boring or bland either; they’re 'safe' and very easy to keep drinking. Personally, I found the Silk Stocking Milk Tea to be just average—but that’s just my take, everyone’s palate is different!
